forge.comments_placeholder: Add any comments about your decision...
forge.apl_empty_description: Approval requests will appear here when runs require approval
forge.create.heading: Author a new mold
forge.create.subtitle: Define a reusable template: identity, inputs, and actions. The right rail renders it live as you build.
forge.create.unsaved_confirm: You have unsaved changes. Leave without creating the mold?
forge.create.created_toast_body: {name} is ready. Open it from the Molds tab.
forge.create.field_slug_help: Lowercase, numbers, and hyphens. Used in URLs and the API.
forge.create.field_description_placehold: What does this mold do? Who is it for?
forge.create.field_icon_help: An icon name (e.g. github, package) or an emoji.
forge.create.visibility_public: Public: every tenant on this Shoehorn instance
forge.create.visibility_help: Controls who can run this mold.
forge.create.inputs_help: Define the parameters runners will fill in. The playground rerenders the form as you type.
forge.create.schema_invalid_json: Invalid JSON. Check brackets and commas.
forge.create.schema_invalid_shape: Schema must be a JSON object.
forge.create.schema_valid: {count, plural, one {1 input field detected.} other {{count} input fields detected.}}
forge.create.actions_help: Buttons runners click to execute the mold. At least one must be marked primary.
forge.create.review_help: Confirm the mold definition. You can edit and republish later.
forge.create.playground_help: How runners will see your mold.
forge.create.playground_empty: Start by giving your mold a name.
forge.create.builder_subtitle: Drag input fields onto the canvas to build the form runners will fill in. The right rail shows the generated mold YAML in real time. That's exactly what gets persisted.
forge.create.discard_confirm: All changes to this mold draft will be lost.
forge.create.palette_help: Click to append a field to the canvas.
forge.create.canvas_help: This is exactly the form runners will fill in.
forge.create.canvas_empty: Start by clicking a palette chip on the left to add a field.
forge.create.canvas_add_hint: Click a palette chip or here to add another field
forge.create.picker_placeholder_catalog: Pick a catalog entity at runtime…
forge.create.action_footer_help: The button runners click to start the run.
forge.create.validation_fields: {count, plural, =0 {No input fields yet} one {1 input field defined} other {{count} input fields defined}}
forge.create.validation_keys_unique: Duplicate field keys. Each key must be unique.
forge.create.friendly_subtitle: Build a reusable template runners can fill out and run. Pick a recipe to start fast, or build it question by question.
forge.create.recipes_help: Pick one to fill in a sensible starting point. You can change everything after.
forge.create.recipe_github_desc: Create a new repo with name and visibility.
forge.create.r_field_repo_name: What should the repo be called?
forge.create.r_field_repo_name_help: Lowercase, hyphens. Used as the GitHub repo name.
forge.create.r_field_visibility_help: Private hides it. Internal is org-only. Public is on the open internet.
forge.create.r_field_init_readme_help: Creates an empty README on the first commit.
forge.create.r_field_service_name_help: Lowercase, hyphens. Becomes the deployment name.
forge.create.r_field_language_help: Picks the scaffold template and CI defaults.
forge.create.r_field_owner_team_help: Team that owns the service after provisioning.
forge.create.r_field_db_name_help: Lowercase, hyphens. Used as the connection database name.
forge.create.r_field_db_engine_help: Determines the connection driver and supported features.
forge.create.r_field_db_size_help: Picks the instance class and storage tier.
forge.create.questions_help: Each question becomes a field on the form runners fill in.
forge.create.questions_empty_help: Add the first thing you want runners to fill in.
forge.create.q_help_placeholder: What this question is asking, in plain language.
forge.create.q_help_required_hint: Add a description for this question.
forge.create.q_key_help: Used to reference the answer in workflow steps.
forge.create.run_button_help: The button runners click to start the run.
forge.create.runner_view_aria: Live preview of the form runners will see
forge.create.runner_view_no_desc: Add a description so runners know what it does.
forge.create.runner_view_empty: Add at least one question to preview the runner form.
forge.create.checklist_fields: {count, plural, =0 {Add at least one question} one {1 question added} other {{count} questions added}}
forge.lay_not_enabled_prefix: The Forge Molds feature is not enabled for your tenant. Contact your administrator to enable the
forge.lay_access_denied: You need developer or platform-engineer privileges. Ask an admin to grant access.
forge.lay_page_desc_molds: Reusable templates for infra and deployments
forge.lay_page_desc_approvals: Review and approve workflow run requests
forge.live_match_count: {count, plural, one {1 mold matches your filters.} other {{count} molds match your filters.}}
forge.live_available_count: {count, plural, one {1 mold available.} other {{count} molds available.}}
forge.molds_search_placeholder: Search Molds by name, description, or tags...
forge.edit.heading: Edit mold
forge.edit.unsaved_confirm: You have unsaved changes. Leaving this page will discard them.
forge.edit.questions_help: These are the inputs runners will fill in when they use this mold.
forge.edit.questions_empty_help: Add a question to capture an input from the runner.
forge.edit.q_question_placeholder: What do you want to ask?
forge.edit.q_help_placeholder: Optional helper text shown beneath the input
forge.edit.q_key_duplicate: Duplicate key. Keys must be unique.
forge.edit.context_help: Slug and version are the natural key. They identify the mold and can't be changed.
forge.edit.checklist_fields: At least one question with a label
forge.edit.actions_help: Buttons runners click after filling in the questions. At least one must be primary.
forge.edit.actions_empty_help: A mold needs at least one action so runners have something to click.
forge.edit.action_id_duplicate: Duplicate action ID. IDs must be unique.
forge.edit.checklist_actions: At least one action with a primary
forge.edit.field_owner_teams_help: Members of these teams can edit this mold in addition to the author. Paste team UUIDs separated by commas.
forge.edit.field_owner_teams_invalid: One or more entries is not a valid UUID.
